summary: Excellent audience recording captures a nostalgic 1984 show with standout Friend Of The Devil, Cumberland Blues and Let It Grow performances.
review: Reviewers rave about the high‑energy first set – especially Friend Of The Devil, Cumberland Blues and Let It Grow – and a second set that delivers Eyes of the World.  The audience source is praised for its clarity and the venue’s great acoustics, making this one of the better‑sounding 1984 crowd recordings.

ai_rating.rationale: Four reviews (average 4.25★) highlight a strong performance and very good audience sound quality; the rating reflects the positive show weight with solid but not exhaustive technical detail.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced capture with nice venue acoustics; praised as the best‑sounding audience source among similar recordings.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as rocking with strong song choices; second set delivers deep‑cut gems like Eyes of the World, creating a satisfying overall pacing.
